zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard de Bois于大约1070年出生在法国皮卡迪奧易布利易爾时，他的父亲Jean de Boissay25岁，母亲Mrs. Jean de Boissay22岁。他和Mrs. Richard de Bois至少有1个儿子。他去世于大约1100年在法国皮卡迪享年32岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bois
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcide, Antoine, Donat, Marcel, Ovide, Ovila, Raoul, Serge.

French: topographic name for someone living or working in a wood, from (Old) French bois ‘wood’ (from Late Latin boscum ‘bush, shrub, undergrowth’, of ancient Germanic origin), or a habitational name from (Le) Bois, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is also found in Haiti. Compare Debois and the most frequent French form Dubois . Compare also Boie , Boies , and Boyce .
English: variant of   Boyce   and   Boyes  .

variants.name: Dubois
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Jacques, Pierre, Fernand, Marcel, Emile, Lucien, Michel, Alain, Luc.

French, Walloon, and English (of French Huguenot origin): topographic name, with fused preposition and definite article du ‘from the’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ), or a habitational name for someone from any of several places in various parts of France and in Belgium (Wallonia) named Le Bois. This is the most frequent surname in Wallonia. It is also found in the Flemish part of Belgium, the Netherlands, Germany, and Haiti. In both England and North America it is sometimes translated into English as Wood . Compare Deboe , Debois , Deboy , Duboise , and Dubose .

History: This surname is listed (in the form Du Bois) in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America. One of the several Huguenot ancestors bearing this name was Chrétien Du Bois from Flanders, whose son, Louis Du Bois (born 1626 in Lille, France, died 1696 in Kingston, NY), was among the Huguenots who founded the town of New Paltz, NY 1675–78.

variants.name: Boisvert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Armand, Andre, Donat, Lucien, Jacques, Gaston, Normand, Pierre, Gilles, Herve, Adrien.
topographic name for someone who lived in a dense forest or perhaps a copse of evergreens, from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + vert, verd ‘green’.

variants.name: Boisclair
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Normand, Marcel, Aldege, Alphonse, Arsene, Edmour, Emile, Leopaul, Prudent, Rejean, Remi, Yves.

French: topographic name from Old French bois ‘wood’ (see Bois ) + clair ‘clear, light’, or a habitational name from (Le) Bois Clair, the name of several places in various parts of France. This surname is virtually non-existing in France.

variants.name: Baskerville
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Boscherville in Eure, France, named with Old Anglo-Norman French boschet ‘copse, thicket’ (a diminutive of Bois ) + ville ‘settlement, town’.

variants.name: Debois
meanings: Altered form of French   Dubois  , a cognate of 1 above.
French: topographic name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, for someone living or working in a wood (see Bois ).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Bois was born about 1070, in Breteuil, Oise, Picardie, France, his father, Jean de Boissay, was 25 and his mother, Mrs. Jean de Boissay, was 22. He had at least 1 son with Mrs. Richard de Bois. He died about 1100, in Picardie, France, at the age of 32.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Bois est né environ 1070, à Breteuil, Oise, Picardie, France, son père, Jean de Boissay, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Jean de Boissay, avait 22 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Mrs. Richard de Bois. Il est décédé environ 1100, à Picardie, France, à l’âge de 32 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Bois um 1070 in Breteuil, Oise, Picardie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jean de Boissay 25 und seine Mutter Mrs. Jean de Boissay 22. Er hatte mit Mrs. Richard de Bois mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb um 1100 im Alter von 32 Jahren in Picardie, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Bois è nato il circa 1070 nel Breteuil, Oise, Picardie, Francia, il suo padre, Jean de Boissay, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. Jean de Boissay, aveva 22 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Mrs. Richard de Bois. Morì circa 1100, nel Picardie, Francia, all'età di 32 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Bois(이)가 대략 1070년 프랑스 피카디 이스 브레테우일에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jean de Boissay(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. Jean de Boissay(은)는 22세였다. 그는 Mrs. Richard de Bois(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1100년 프랑스 피카디에서 32세의 나이로 사망했다.

Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Mrs. Richard de Bois. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1100, em Picardie, França, com 32 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Bois родился около 1070 г. в местности бретеуил, оис, пикарди, Франция, его отцу по имени Jean de Boissay было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Jean de Boissay, 22. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Mrs. Richard de Bois. Он умер около 1100 г. в местности пикарди, Франция в возрасте 32 лет.
